title: UNIQUE(persona_id, source_ref) allows NULL source_ref duplicates
description: The schema declares UNIQUE(persona_id, source_ref) on the posts table (db.rs:35). SQLite treats NULL as not equal to any other value, including another NULL. This means INSERT OR IGNORE will not deduplicate rows where source_ref IS NULL: every call to post::create() with source_ref=None inserts a fresh row, regardless of identical content. This is the expected SQLite behavior but is a semantic footgun: the CLI 'post' command and webhook handler both pass source_ref=None, so two identical CLI posts or two identical webhook calls produce two rows. Users may find this surprising. The watch.rs handler uses the file path as source_ref (correct); the feed poller uses entry ID (correct). Worth documenting the behavior in a comment, or enforcing a content hash as fallback source_ref for webhook/CLI paths.
close_reason: Pass 3 fixes: HMAC key compare, inbox SSRF guard, domain validation, async Accept dispatch, foreign keys, IPv6 SSRF, poll timeout. Remaining P2-P3 accepted as known debt.
